behaviors.description: Migrations are applied in ascending numeric order determined by the filename prefix.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module boundaries (directory discovery -> filename parsing/sort -> DB apply order), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement beyond local filesystem + local DB, so Integration is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking DB/files can miss handoff/order bugs between modules. Rejection gate: System was considered, but behavior text only requires ordering by filename prefix and apply sequence; it does not require subprocess/OS-specific effects.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp migrations directory with out-of-order filenames (e.g., 010, 002, 001), run against a local Postgres test DB, then assert execution order from _migrations rows/timestamps (or sequence) is 001 -> 002 -> 010.

behaviors.description: A migration that has already been applied is not re-executed on a subsequent run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is about persisted state across runs (_migrations metadata + runner logic), so single-function tests are insufficient. REAL-INFRA: local DB/file wiring is enough; no real external network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing metadata checks can hide real idempotency defects in read/write coordination. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text is about subsequent run state in the same system, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run runner once with a migration that creates a marker table/row; run runner again against same DB and same files; assert migration SQL side effects occurred once and _migrations has one record for that version.

behaviors.description: When a migration statement fails, its changes are rolled back automatically and no further migrations in the same run are attempted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: rollback + stop-on-failure is transaction and control-flow behavior across runner, DB client, and migration sequencing modules. REAL-INFRA: requires real transactional behavior from local Postgres, but not OS/subprocess-level effects. MOCK-HIDE: mocked transaction success/failure often misses true rollback semantics and partial-write edge cases. Rejection gate: System considered, but behavior text does not require subprocess orchestration or external network conditions; local DB integration captures the real failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Use migration 001 with a valid write, migration 002 containing a failing statement after an initial write, and migration 003 valid. Run runner once and assert: writes from 002 are absent (rolled back), 003 not applied, and _migrations records only successful prior versions.

behaviors.description: When two instances of the runner start at the same time against the same database, each migration is applied exactly once across both instances.
behaviors.justification: INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) yes, failure boundary explicitly involves separate processes starting simultaneously, which is OS/subprocess concurrency; Integration is too low. (b) no AI/ML dependency. (c) no multi-agentic chain. MOCK-HIDE: in-process concurrency tests can miss inter-process locking/race behavior. Rejection gate: Integration rejected by concrete text 'two instances ... start at the same time' and 'across both instances', which requires real process-level contention.
behaviors.test_description: Start two runner processes concurrently (separate Node subprocesses) against the same Postgres DB and identical migration set; wait for both to finish; assert all migrations are present exactly once in _migrations and SQL side effects are not duplicated.

behaviors.description: On the very first run against a fresh database, the _migrations table is created before any migration file is applied.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this validates initialization ordering across schema-bootstrap logic and migration execution modules. REAL-INFRA: local DB integration is sufficient; no real subprocess/network requirement. MOCK-HIDE: mocked metadata repository can hide ordering bugs where migration applies before table creation. Rejection gate: System rejected because behavior text only requires first-run DB bootstrap ordering, not OS-level behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a fresh Postgres schema with no _migrations table, run runner with at least one migration; assert _migrations exists and includes first migration entry, and no migration execution occurred before table creation (e.g., verified by audit/log hook or by requiring metadata insert to succeed before migration commit path).
